checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?
Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: GrossProfit=263,997,037.43 (FY2011) on SalesRevenueNet=347,545,296.99 (FY2011) ~76.0% margin, consistent with GrossProfit=259,706,354.75/SalesRevenueNet=338,770,646.82 (FY2010) ~76.7% margin

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?
Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?
Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?
Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data provided from 2008-12-31 through 2012-09-30 across Assets, Liabilities, Revenue, NetIncomeLoss, cash flow and receivable/inventory/goodwill line items

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential delayed revenue recognition timing or insufficient reserving against slowing collections: receivables grew ~9.7% YoY (Q3 2011 to Q3 2012) while quarterly revenue declined ~2.2% over the same period, and the receivables-to-annualized-revenue ratio rose from roughly 30% (Q3 2011) to roughly 34% (Q3 2012), suggesting either channel stuffing, extended payment terms, or under-provisioning for doubtful accounts relative to a slowing top line.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=85,355,652.61 (2011-09-30) rising to 93,627,882.33 (2012-09-30) while SalesRevenueNet fell from 70,414,496.69 (Q3 2011) to 68,881,424.00 (Q3 2012)
rationale_evidence.location: 10-Q balance sheet and income statement, periods 2011-09-30 and 2012-09-30

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Possible 'big bath' or aggressive one-time charge recognized in Q1 2011 (large unexplained gap between gross profit trend and reported operating loss, not fully explained by disclosed G&A/R&D line items), driving a full-year 2011 net loss despite strong full-year operating cash flow generation - a pattern consistent with a large non-cash write-off/reserve being used to depress earnings in one period while cash flow remained robust, which can mask underlying operating performance or be reversed/smoothed in later periods.

rationale_evidence.location: 10-K income statement and cash flow statement

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Apparent retrospective reclassification/restatement associated with a divestiture or discontinued-operations presentation: goodwill and intangibles dropped abruptly by roughly $64M and $17-19M respectively between Q1 2012 and Q2 2012, and the same large reduction was retroactively applied to the previously reported 2011-12-31 balance (restated Goodwill=43,875,651.89 vs an originally implied ~107-108M trend), accompanied by a corresponding collapse in secured long-term debt and total liabilities/assets - raising risk of inconsistent or delayed disclosure of a material transaction and reduced comparability of prior-period figures.

top_signals: Receivables grew ~9.7% YoY (Q3 2011 to Q3 2012) while revenue declined ~2.2%, pushing implied DSO from ~30% to ~34% of annualized revenue

FY2011 net loss (NetIncomeLoss=-644,082.61) coexisted with strong operating cash flow (OCF=38,885,662.37), driven by an unexplained large Q1 2011 operating loss (-15.6M) inconsistent with surrounding quarters
Abrupt, large retrospective restatement of Goodwill/Intangibles/Debt/Assets/Liabilities between Q1 2012 and Q2 2012 filings, with prior-period (2011-12-31) balances also revised downward materially
Recurring history of 10-K/A amendments in 2004, 2007, 2009, 2011, and 2012, indicating a pattern of post-filing corrections
Modest decline in accrued liabilities (10.75M to 9.64M) despite revenue growth from FY2010 to FY2011
